content:
# Service Request Workflow

This document outlines the end-to-end workflow for processing a customer service request within a transport customer portal.

## Overview

When a customer submits a service request, the system routes it through identity validation, eligibility checking, queue assignment, and resolution steps. Each stage has defined decision points and fallback paths.

## Process Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A([Customer submits request]) --> B{Validate identity}
    B -- Valid --> C{Check eligibility}
    B -- Invalid --> D[/Return 401 error/]
    C -- Eligible --> E[Assign to service queue]
    C -- Ineligible --> F[Send ineligibility notice]
    E --> G{Agent available?}
    G -- Yes --> H[Assign agent]
    G -- No --> I[Queue & notify ETA]
    H --> J[Process request]
    I --> J
    J --> K{Resolution type}
    K -- Resolved --> L([Close & send confirmation])
    K -- Escalate --> M[Escalate to Tier 2]
    M --> J
```

## Key Decision Points

| Stage | Decision | Outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Identity | Valid credentials | Proceed to eligibility check |
| Identity | Invalid credentials | Return 401 and log attempt |
| Eligibility | Meets criteria | Route to service queue |
| Eligibility | Fails criteria | Send automated notice with reason |
| Assignment | Agent available | Immediate assignment |
| Assignment | No agent available | Queue with ETA notification |
| Resolution | Resolved at Tier 1 | Close ticket and send confirmation |
| Resolution | Needs escalation | Escalate to Tier 2 and continue |

## Implementation Notes

- All identity checks integrate with the **OAuth2/OIDC provider** via the auth middleware
- Queue assignment uses weighted round-robin across active agents
- SLA timers begin when a request enters the queue, not at submission
- Tier 2 escalations increment the request priority and notify supervisors via webhook
- All state transitions are persisted to the audit log for compliance reporting
